title: Sorting, Race by Race
tagline: Five algorithms, one array — watch insertion, bubble, merge, quick, and heap sort run side by side
summary: Bar-chart sorting visualisations are everywhere, but few let you race the algorithms head-to-head on identical input. Five classic sorts step in lockstep on the same array; counters track comparisons and writes; toggle the input distribution to see why quicksort's worst case is sorted input, why insertion sort wins on tiny arrays, and why Timsort took over the standard libraries.
highlights: Five algorithms race in lockstep on the same array: insertion, bubble, mergesort, quicksort, heapsort.
Live comparison and write counters expose the constant factors that asymptotics hide.
Toggle distribution (random / nearly-sorted / reversed / sorted) — watch quicksort's worst case appear and insertion sort win on small arrays.
Pivot strategies side by side: first-element vs random vs median-of-three on the same adversarial input.
Why Timsort/quicksort beat heapsort despite identical asymptotics: cache locality, branch prediction, run detection.
